extra.reasoning: The input variable `pallor` does not appear in any rule's condition or action. The 12 rules (lines 21-32) reference only the other 12 inputs (leg_swelling, confusion, back_pain, abdominal_pain, palpitations, hemoptysis, chest_pain, focal_weakness, slurred_speech, dizziness, stiff_neck, rash). The acuity output is therefore a pure function of those 12 variables plus the default rule. Toggling `pallor` from false to true, with all other inputs fixed, does not change the set of matching rules, their priorities, the tie-breaking order, or the default — so the computed acuity is exactly identical before and after. Identical output trivially satisfies "no lower than before" (the monotonicity property). Additionally, every rule only raises acuity to `urgent` or `emergent`; none ever lowers it, and the only path to `routine` is the default when no rule fires — but pallor never influences whether any rule fires. Hence the property holds across the entire input space (2^13 assignments). File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/triage-d12-hold-117.txt
